sentences: Here, e is the input embedding that is fed as input to the decoder.
The box is a 4D vector <MATH_079> , having the box center value as <MATH_080> , width value as box <MATH_081> and height value as boxh.
The <MATH_082> function normalizes the predictions and varies from 0 to 1.
<ENT_081>() predicts the un-normalized box. r is the un-normalized 2D coordinate of the reference-point, and (0, 0) is the simple <ENT_008>.
This work either learns the reference point r for each box or generates it from the respective object query.
It learns queries for multi-head cross-attention from input embeddings of the decoder.
This spatial query makes the cross-attention head consider the explicit region, which helps to localize the different regions for class labels and bounding boxes by narrowing down the spatial range.
